data.title: India is said to expand airport closures amid rising tensions with Pakistan
data.description:
India has broadened its suspension of civilian air traffic to include more airports near its western border, as tensions with Pakistan intensify following recent military action, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the situation. The Indian government has now restricted civilian operations at more than 25 airports, up from the previously announced 16.

Among the newly restricted facilities is a private airstrip in Mundra, operated by the Adani Group. Other affected airports are located in strategic northern and western cities such as Chandigarh, Shimla, Srinagar, Leh, Amritsar, Jamnagar, Jaisalmer and Porbandar. Mundra serves as an entry point to India's largest private port, while Jamnagar is key for Reliance Industries Ltd., home to one of the world’s largest oil refineries.

The expanded restrictions indicate a more cautious posture by Indian authorities than in previous military escalations with Pakistan. By limiting civilian aircraft operations in the region, Indian defense forces aim to maintain clearer airspace for monitoring and rapid response in case of retaliatory strikes from Islamabad, sources said.

India claims to have launched airstrikes on nine locations in Pakistan-administered territory as a response to a deadly attack in Pahalgam that left 26 civilians dead. Pakistan, meanwhile, has reported casualties including women and children and has vowed to respond at a time of its choosing.

This current move contrasts with India’s approach during the 2019 Balakot airstrikes, when only limited and temporary airspace restrictions were imposed, mostly around Srinagar and nearby regions.

Energy infrastructure

Now, authorities have also closed Bhatinda airport, which services another major oil facility co-owned by Hindustan Petroleum and Mittal Energy Investment.

The Airports Authority of India and the Adani Group haven't issued statements regarding the closures.

The growing hostilities have prompted international airlines to adjust routes. United Airlines (UAL) has suspended flights to India, while several European carriers have rerouted to avoid potential conflict zones.

These diversions, coupled with ongoing avoidance of Russian airspace due to the Ukraine conflict, have significantly increased flight times -- by up to 100 minutes on some long-haul routes -- raising operational costs due to increased fuel consumption.

data.title: Arm Holdings in focus as Wall Street largely defends despite weak forecast
data.description:
Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) was in focus on Thursday as Wall Street analysts largely defended the British chip design firm after it issued a weaker-than-expected outlook for the first-quarter of fiscal 2026.

Shares fell slightly more than 8% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

Morgan Stanley analyst Lee Simpson, who has an Overweight rating and $150 price target on Arm, said the fiscal fourth-quarter results were good, but the higher-than-expected operating expenses could mean higher demand later. 

“A higher opex for Q4 ($566m, up 11% [year-over-year]) didn't stop the company giving a higher adjusted operating margin for Q4 after all (52.8% vs. 50%, using midpoint of sales guide),” Simpson wrote in a note to clients. “And yet guide to $625m in Q1 on lower sales suggests margins tick down again (as expected), which we think means Arm is building its engineering base quickly, given the demand to come.”

Simpson continued: “The shareholder letter also talks about AI design momentum, as well as broadening of use of Arm in edge AI and design momentum in automotive and data centers to support growth this coming year. In the meantime, the company gave broad commentary of CSS/v9 take up, but hasn't as yet given a guide for FY26, fueling fears of slower earnings momentum, given the ongoing investment phase (higher opex). All the same, we expect Arm to still make solid progress through this year, given a broad range of drivers.”

Wells Fargo analyst Joe Quatrochi, who has an Overweight rating on Arm, noted he's still positive on Arm's forward-looking commentary, and its “position to benefit from emerging AI [opportunities].”

J.P. Morgan analyst Harlan Sur, who also rates Arm Overweight, lowered his price target to $140 from $175 as he sees the potential for some of Arm's largest customers — such as Nvidia (NVDA) — to move to custom ARM cores, which could impact royalty rates.

“We believe these customers are driving potentially better performance out of their custom cores and potentially better economics (lower royalties) versus off-the-shelf/CSS — based solutions,” Sur wrote. “Even if royalty rates are not that different, these customers still appear to be driving better price/performance metrics with custom solutions — a value proposition that ARM was supposed to provide with its off-the-shelf high-performance cores combined with its CSS solutions. This is a dynamic we will continue to monitor.”

Several other Wall Street firms cut their price targets on Arm following the results and guidance, including HSBC, TD Cowen, KeyBanc, Rosenblatt and Raymond James.

data.title: Axogen names Lindsey Hartley as finance chief
data.description:
• Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) said it has appointed Lindsey Hartley as its chief financial officer, effective May 12.
  • Hartley currently serves as vice president and corporate controller at Axogen and will succeed Nir Naor.
  • Naor will remain with the company in an advisory capacity until July 1.
  • Hartley has more than 19 years of financial leadership experience and had joined the firm in October 2021.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health to offer $450M convertible senior notes
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) plans to offer $450M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.

The notes will accrue interest payable semi-annually in arrears and will mature on May 15, 2030, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted.

The interest rate, initial conversion rate and other terms of the notes will be determined at the pricing of the offering.

The company also expects to grant the initial purchasers an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of 13 days from, and including, the date the notes are first issued, up to an additional $67.5M of notes.

Hims & Hers (NYSE:HIMS) intends to use proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including accelerating global expansion through both organic growth and strategic acquisitions.

Shares were down 3.95% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Black Diamond Group to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Black Diamond Group (OTCQX:BDIMF) said the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved its intention to renew its normal course issuer bid.
  • The bid will start on May 12, and in the 12-month period, the firm can purchase up to 4.5M shares.

data.title: Papa John's gains after earnings topper, reiterated full-year sale guidance
data.description:
Papa John's International (NASDAQ:PZZA) gained in early trading on Thursday after turning in a better-than-feared earnings report in the face of consumer discretionary spending headwinds.

North America comparable sales were down 2.7% year-over-year to match the consensus expectation. Comparable sales for domestic company-owned restaurants were down 5% and were down 2% for North America franchised restaurants. International comparable sales were up 3% during the quarter.

Global system-wide restaurant sales were up 1% to $1.22 billion, as higher international comparable sales and trailing twelve-month net restaurant growth were partially offset by lower North American comparable sales.

Adjusted EBITDA fell to $49.6 million from $60.6 million a year ago. EPS came in at $0.36 vs. $0.35 consensus and $0.67 a year ago. Higher G&A expenses related to incremental marketing and loyalty investments to support the company’s brand positioning work and the biannual franchisee conference were a drag on profits during the quarter.

"Our strategic investments in marketing and technology are driving early momentum in the business, and customers are responding positively to our strengthened value proposition and enhanced digital and loyalty experiences, as evidenced by sequential improvement in comparable sales and transactions," stated CEO Todd Penegor.

Looking ahead, the pizza chain operator reiterated its full-year guidance for system-wide sales to be up 2% to 5%. Adjusted EBITDA of $200 million to $220 million is anticipated for the full year.

Shares of Papa John's (NASDAQ:PZZA) were up 2.1% in premarket action to cut into the 19% year-to-date decline.
